title: Factory Config
description: Project-local configuration for Factory tooling, personas, verification, and pipeline behavior.

persona_providers.description: Each persona maps to either a single provider name (legacy single-string form, kept for backward compatibility) OR an ordered list of provider names representing the cross-CLI failover order. Phase 7 of single-entry-pipeline.

cost_caps.description: Phase 5.7 — optional dollar caps per scope. Absent = disabled (no enforcement). Caps use >= semantics: a running total at-or-above the cap triggers escalation.

per_run.description: Per-run cap in USD. Crossing aborts the entire pipeline run.

per_packet.description: Per-packet cap in USD. Crossing fails just the affected packet; the orchestrator continues to the next independent packet.

per_day.description: Per-day cap in USD (LOCAL date). Crossing aborts the run AND records the date so subsequent same-day runs are blocked at orchestrator entry.

rate_card.description: Phase 5.7 — optional partial overrides on top of DEFAULT_RATE_CARD in tools/pipeline/cost.ts. Outer key is provider, inner key is model id.

heartbeat_interval_ms.description: Optional heartbeat cadence in milliseconds for long-running agent invocations (planner / developer / reviewer / qa). The first heartbeat fires after this many milliseconds of the child still running, then every interval after. Absent => 30000 ms (the pre-configurable default). Minimum 1000 (1 s).

persona_provider_value.description: Either a single provider name (single-string form) or an ordered list of provider names (cross-CLI failover order). Phase 7 of single-entry-pipeline.

prefix_args.description: DEP0190 shell removal. Optional fixed leading argv elements prepended to every invocation of this provider (before the per-provider suffix flags). Used when a provider CLI requires sub-command-style invocation (e.g. 'gh copilot --' splits into command='gh' + prefix_args=['copilot', '--']). When absent, the per-provider argv is built from the suffix flags alone. Each element is a literal argv token — no shell tokenization, no whitespace splitting. The legacy single-string form (command containing whitespace) is still accepted by the loader for backward compatibility but emits a deprecation warning. See specs/dep0190-shell-removal.md.

model_failover.description: Phase 7 of single-entry-pipeline. Optional within-CLI model failover order for ABSTRACTION providers (those that route to multiple underlying models, e.g. copilot). Each element is a model id; the first element is tried first, then the next on failure, and so on. Direct providers (codex, claude — one CLI maps to one upstream provider) do NOT set this field; the absence of model_failover means 'on failure, fall through to the next CLI in persona_providers without trying alternate models'.
